reasoning: The informal statement makes two claims about f(x) = x² + 1 over ℤ₂: (1) that f has exactly one zero (uniqueness), and (2) that this unique zero is x = 1. The Lean statement `Polynomial.IsRoot f 1` unfolds (per `Mathlib/Algebra/Polynomial/Eval.lean`) to `f.eval 1 = 0`, which only asserts that 1 is a root of f. It does not assert that 1 is the only root. Thus the Lean formalization captures only part of the informal claim — it drops the uniqueness ("exactly one zero") part. The informal statement implies the Lean statement, but not vice versa, so the Lean encoding is strictly weaker.
